title: Chapter 1 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Kotlin basics!

questions.question: Who created the Kotlin programming language?
questions.explanation: Kotlin was created by JetBrains, the company behind many popular IDEs like IntelliJ IDEA.

questions.question: Which tech giant officially supports Kotlin as their preferred language for Android development?
questions.explanation: Google announced Kotlin as the officially supported and preferred language for Android apps in 2017.

questions.question: Kotlin can only be used to build Android applications.
questions.explanation: Kotlin is versatile! It can be used for backend servers, web applications, data science, and desktop apps.

questions.question: Kotlin is 100% interoperable with which older, legacy language?
questions.explanation: Kotlin is fully interoperable with Java, allowing you to run them side-by-side in the same project.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to declare a function in Kotlin?
questions.explanation: Kotlin uses the concise 'fun' keyword to declare new functions.

questions.question: Every Kotlin program execution begins at a specific entry point. What is the name of this function?
questions.explanation: The 'main()' function serves as the entry point where exactly your program begins taking action.

questions.question: Use the _____ keyword to define a function in Kotlin.
questions.explanation: Kotlin uses the 'fun' keyword.

questions.question: What function is used to print text to the console and automatically move to the next line?
questions.explanation: 'println()' displays output and moves the cursor to a new line.

questions.question: Semicolons (;) are strictly required at the end of every statement in Kotlin.
questions.explanation: Unlike Java, Kotlin does not require semicolons at the end of standard statements. This makes the code cleaner!

questions.question: How do you start a single-line comment in Kotlin?
questions.explanation: Two forward slashes '//' initiate a single-line comment.

questions.question: How do you begin a multi-line comment block?
questions.explanation: You open a multi-line block with '/*'.

questions.question: How do you close a multi-line comment block?
questions.explanation: You conclude a multi-line block with '*/'.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of writing comments in your code?
options: To make the code run faster
To leave explanatory notes for humans
questions.explanation: Comments are completely ignored by the computer. They exist simply for humans to read and understand the thought process behind the code.

questions.question: The print() function moves the cursor to the next line after outputting the text.
questions.explanation: print() stays on the same line. println() moves to the next line.

questions.question: To write 'Hello' to the screen, we type: _____("Hello")
questions.explanation: The print or println functions display output.

questions.question: Which of these lines of code is a valid way to display the number 5?
questions.explanation: You place the content to print inside the parentheses ().

questions.question: Kotlin's syntax is generally more verbose (longer) than Java's.
questions.explanation: Kotlin was designed to be concise, meaning it usually requires far less code than Java to achieve the same result.

questions.question: What punctuation marks envelop the code block inside a function?
questions.explanation: Functions use curly braces `{ }` to enclose the block of instructions.

questions.question: The starting point of a Kotlin app is the _____ function.
questions.explanation: The main() function is the entry point.

questions.question: Which one of these does the compiler completely ignore during execution?
questions.explanation: Comments are stripped out and ignored by the compiler when the code is executed.
